rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Motion Ofr Summary Judgement Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: (PART 2 OF 2 - WILLIAMS TRUST) The Trust at issue in the instant matter became irrevocable on the death of the surviving spouse, Leroy Williams, on June 11, 2021. (UMF, Nos. 5, 6; Taylor Decl., 3, Exh. A [Article 5.C.(5)]; Williams Decl., 7-8, Exhs. 3-4.) On July 23, 2021, approximately 42 days after the death of Mr. Williams, Respondent served Petitioner, by first class mail, with notice. (UMF No. 11; Taylor Decl., 6, Exh. C.) The notice, titled "Notification by Trustee," identifies the trust, the settlors and the date of execution, the trustee, the place of administration of the trust, and a notification that the recipient is entitled to a copy of the trust, all as required by section 16061.7, subdivision (g). (Ibid.) It further provides in boldface the notification of the statute of limitations required in section 16061.7, subdivision (h). (Ibid.) A copy of the Trust was provided to Petitioner on July 20, 2021. (UMF No. 9; Taylor Decl., 5, Exh. B.) Emails from Petitioner confirm receipt of the copy of the Trust. (UMF Nos. 10, 13, 14; Williams Decl., 10, Exh. 5; Taylor Decl. 7, Exh. D.) Petitioner filed her Petition on September 5, 2023-almost 2 years after the notification by trustee was served on her. (UMF No. 16.) Based on the evidence before the Court, summary judgment is appropriate here. The 120-day deadline for Petitioner to file her Petition to contest the provisions of the Trust expired on November 22, 2021. Accordingly, Petitioner's trust contest filed on September 5, 2023 is untimely and time-barred by the limitation period set forth in section 16061.8. Respondent's Motion for Summary Judgment is GRANTED. Respondent shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204. (END OF TENTATIVE RULING)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE WILLIAMS FAMILY TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: (PART 1 OF 2 - WILLIAMS TRUST) This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Stephen M. Murphy. Before the court is Respondent Shawny Williams' Motion for Summary Judgment, or in the alternative, Summary Adjudication to Petitioner Shanda Love's Petition for Order to Invalidate The Williams Family Trust of Betty Yvonne Williams and Leroy Williams and to Remove the Appointed Trustee, Shawny K. Williams Under Conspiracy, Fraud, Duress, Undue Influence, Altering of a Will Under an Affidavit of Death and Appoint Plaintiff as New Trustee Pursuant to California Probate Code Section, 15642, 15643, 17200 California Welfare Institutions Code 15610.70. No opposition to the motion was filed. The Motion was brought on two grounds: (1) Petitioner's failure to file her Petition within the statutory time frame set forth in Probate Code section 16061.8; and (2) Petitioner's lack of standing to seek removal of trustee under Probate Code section 17200. Because the Court finds that Petitioner's contest of the Trust is barred by the applicable statute of limitations, it does not reach the issue of whether Petitioner lacks standing to challenge the testamentary instrument. A defendant has met his or her burden of showing that a cause of action has no merit if the party has shown that there is a complete defense to the cause of action. (Code Civ. Proc. SEC 437c(p)(2); Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield Co. (2001) 25 Cal.4th 826, 849.) Once the defendant has met that burden, the burden shifts to the plaintiff to show that a triable issue of one or more material facts exists as to the cause of action or a defense thereto. (Ibid.) Probate Code section 16061.7 provides, as relevant here, that a trustee shall, within 60 days, notify the beneficiaries and heirs of the deceased settlors when a revocable trust becomes irrevocable because of the death of the settlor of the trust. (Id. at subds. (a), (b), (f).) "The notification by trustee shall contain the following information: [] (1) The identity of the settlor ... and the date of execution of the trust instrument. [] (2) The name, mailing address and telephone number of each trustee of the trust. [] (3) The address of the physical location where the principal place of administration of the trust is located.... [] (4) Any additional information that may be expressly required by the terms of the trust instrument. [] (5) A notification that the recipient is entitled, upon reasonable request to the trustee, to receive from the trustee a true and complete copy of the terms of the trust." (Id. at subd. (g).) If, as here, the notification by the trustee is served because a trust has become irrevocable upon the death of a settlor, the notification must also "include a warning, set out in a separate paragraph in not less than 10-point boldface type, or a reasonable equivalent thereof, that states as follows: [] 'You may not bring an action to contest the trust more than 120 days from the date this notification by the trustee is served upon you or 60 days from the date on which a copy of the terms of the trust is mailed or personally delivered to you during that 120-day period, whichever is later.' " (Probate Code SEC 16061.7, subd. (h).) Probate Code section 16061.8 states that no person "upon whom the notification by the trustee is served" may bring an action more than 120 days after service of the notification or 60 days after a copy of the terms of the trust is mailed or delivered to him or her during the 120-day period, "whichever is later." (SEE PART 2 OF 2)
